zh.lifeSummary: Sarah Rogers是Richard Rogers和Barbara Chandler的女兒，出生在英國艾可維瑟斯費爾德。她於大約1598年與John Stone在英國艾可維瑟斯費爾德結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於1629年在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州哈特福德恩費爾德。

nameMeanings.name: Rogers
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of Roger , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.
Irish: adopted for Gaelic Mac Ruaidhrí (see Rorie ).

History: Thomas Rogers (c. 1587–1621), born in London, England, was among the Pilgrim Fathers who sailed on the Mayflower in 1620. He died during the first winter at Plymouth Colony, but his son Joseph survived and married, and was later joined in MA by his brother John. This name was subsequently brought to North America independently by many different bearers.

variants.name: Roger
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Philippe, Alcee, Armand, Laurent, Leonce, Marcel, Yves, Clovis, Fernand, Gilberte, Janvier, Jean Claude.

Scottish, English, North German, French, Walloon, Catalan, and West Indian (mainly Haiti): from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements hrōd ‘renown’ +gār, gēr ‘spear, lance’, which was introduced into England by the Normans in the form Rog(i)er and was reinforced by the Old Norse cognate Hróthgeirr.

History: Gabriel Roger from Sainte-Verge in Deux-Sèvres, France, married Marie (de) Lacour in Quebec City, QC, in 1669. — This surname is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of South Carolina.

variants.name: Rodgers
meanings: English and Irish: variant of   Rogers  .

nameMeanings.name: Sarah
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, borne by the wife of Abraham and mother of Isaac. According to the Book of Genesis, she was originally called Sarai (possibly meaning ‘contentious’ in Hebrew), but had her name changed by God to the more auspicious Sarah ‘princess’ in token of a greater blessing (Genesis 17:15, ‘And God said unto Abraham, As for Sarai thy wife, thou shalt not call her name Sarai, but Sarah shall her name be’). This has been one of the most enduringly popular girls' names.

variants.name: Sara
meanings: Variant of   Sarah .  This is the form used in the Greek of the New Testament (Hebrews 11:11).

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: Sarah Rogers was born in Wethersfield, Essex, England as the daughter of Richard Rogers and Barbara Chandler. She married John Stone about 1598, in Wethersfield, Essex, England. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 4 daughters. She died in 1629, in Enfield, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America.

ko.lifeSummary: Sarah Rogers(은)는 영국 에세스 w더스필드에서 Richard Rogers(와)과 Barbara Chandler의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1598년 영국 에세스 w더스필드에서 John Stone(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1629년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 하트퍼드 필드에서 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Sarah Rogers родилась в местности ветирсфиелд, эссекс, Англия. Её родителей звали Richard Rogers и Barbara Chandler. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Stone около 1598 г. в местности ветирсфиелд, эссекс, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 4 дочери. Она умерла в 1629 г. в местности энфиелд, хартфорд, Коннектикут, бритиш колоняль амирика.
